authentication: Users must be logged in to checkout
Guest checkout should be available for faster purchases
All purchases require user authentication for security
payment: Support credit cards, debit cards, and PayPal
Payment processing must complete within 3 seconds
Complex fraud detection may take up to 10 seconds
Store payment methods for future use
Never store credit card information (PCI compliance)
shipping: Offer standard (5-7 days) and express (2-3 days) shipping
Free express shipping for premium members
inventory: Reserve items in cart for 15 minutes
Items should remain in cart indefinitely until checkout
Allow overselling by 10% to maximize revenue
Never oversell to maintain customer trust
confirmation: Tracking numbers generated 24 hours after order
returns: All items eligible for return within 30 days

contradictions.req1: Users must be logged in to checkout
contradictions.req2: Guest checkout should be available for faster purchases
contradictions.reason: Direct contradiction between mandatory login and guest checkout availability.

contradictions.req1: Store payment methods for future use
contradictions.req2: Never store credit card information (PCI compliance)
contradictions.reason: Conflicting instructions on storing payment data.

contradictions.req1: Allow overselling by 10% to maximize revenue
contradictions.req2: Never oversell to maintain customer trust

clarifying_questions: Should guest checkout be allowed, or is user authentication mandatory for all purchases?
Can we store payment tokens for future use while remaining PCI compliant by not storing actual credit card numbers?
Is the policy to allow 10% overselling or to never oversell under any circumstances?
